Content is not allowed in prolog: Check for whitespaces, blank lines, or hidden characters before the <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> tag at the very top of the file. The file must start exactly with '<'.
could not find field with name: This occurs if a field is implicitly defined via auto-parameters but used inside a complex <field-layout>. Explicitly define the field (e.g., <field name="yourField"><default-field><hidden/></default-field></field>) BEFORE the <field-layout> block.
formInstance was null: CRITICAL: Caused by placing widgets directly inside a <field-layout>. You CANNOT use widgets like <container>, <display-entity>, <label>, or <link> directly inside field-layout. Define the widget inside a <field> tag first, then reference it using <field-ref name="yourField"/>.
Template Error: Often caused by placing widgets directly inside <field-layout> or missing a closing tag. Check that <field-layout> only contains <field-col-row>, <field-col>, or <field-group>. To use a widget, define it inside a <field> tag first, then reference it using <field-ref>.
Doing nothing for element: Occurs when an XML tag is used in a context where the Moqui renderer doesn't support it (e.g., trying to use a raw Quasar <q-card> or <row> tag). Ensure you are using Moqui-compatible tags like <container style="row q-card">.
condition attribute invalid: Adding condition="..." directly to a <link> or <container> tag is invalid according to the Moqui screen XSD. To conditionally render these, wrap the widget inside a <section name="SomeName" condition="..."> element.
Persistent Tabs in UI: If tabs remain on the screen after changing a subscreens-panel to 'popup', it is likely a browser Vue/Quasar cache issue. Instruct the user to perform a hard refresh (Cmd+Shift+R) in their browser.
Empty Form Lists: Verify that <field-ref> is used inside your <form-list-column>s. Also, ensure the 'list' attribute in the <form-list> exactly matches the list name produced by the <entity-find> in the <actions> block.
null appearing in UI: A defensive programming failure. In the <actions> block, use Groovy's null-safe operator (?.) or Elvis operator (?:) to set a default label if a lookup fails. Example: statusDesc = statusItem?.description ?: 'Not Set'
Link disabled in ScreenTestRender: If a screen with a 'Close View' or 'Back' link is rendered in a ScreenTest, the link might appear disabled in the HTML output. Ensure the lastScreenUrl passed to the test resolves to a valid absolute or relative path.
Invalid URL or Routing errors: Internal links across modules are failing. Always set a consistent appRoot variable in the <always-actions> of your main parent screen to simplify links (e.g., url="//${appRoot}/Module/Screen"), and ensure you are using relative paths (../../) correctly.
java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: Moqui system artifacts or build classes are corrupt. Execute './gradlew cleanAll' followed by './gradlew loadSave' to rebuild and restore the data snapshot.
Unique index or primary key violation: Data integrity error, likely due to reloading data over existing records or sequence collisions. Run './gradlew cleanDb loadSave' to reset the environment state.
MissingPropertyException: No such proper: CRITICAL: Occurs when trying to access 'urlMap' directly from 'sri.screenUrlInfo'. This property is not public or does not exist. Use 'sri.screenUrlInfo.targetScreen?.getScreenName()' to safely check the destination screen name instead.
Stale Entity Data after Status Transitio: When a service transitions an entity's status (e.g., LcStatus), subsequent logic in the same action block may still see the OLD status in local value-fields. FIX: Call <entity-find-one entity-name="..." value-field="..."/> immediately after the status transition service call to refresh the local record from the database.
Missing Transaction Status Seed Data: If a transaction (LcTransaction) cannot transition to 'Approved', check '10_TradeFinanceData.xml' for missing status flow definitions (e.g., Draft -> Submitted -> Approved). Without these definitions, transition services will fail silently or throw validation errors.
